summaryText: Guest Speaker Emma Mullings opened by sharing her powerful testimony of loss, abuse, and rebellion before encountering Jesus at age 22. She then taught from Judges 4, focusing on the stories of Deborah and Jael, who God used to defeat the oppressive ruler Sisera and restore Israel to its 'land flowing with milk and honey.' Pastor Emma declared that God has a promised land of purpose for every believer, but an enemy—a 'Sisera' of addiction, fear, shame, or past trauma—often stands in the way. The core message was a call to 'Pioneer again,' to take new ground in our lives, families, and destinies. She outlined three attributes of a pioneer: not looking back at the past, being radically obedient to the Holy Spirit in the present, and taking bold steps of faith toward the future. The sermon concluded with an altar call for salvation and a prayer for strength, emphasizing that Jesus is the ultimate pioneer who provides pardon for our past, peace for our present, and a promise for our tomorrow.
mainPoints: God has a 'land flowing with milk and honey'—a purpose and promise—for each person, but an enemy (a 'Sisera') often tries to block it and must be confronted.
A pioneer is characterized by three things: refusing to look back at the past, being radically obedient to the Holy Spirit in the present, and taking bold steps of faith toward the future.
Jesus is the ultimate pioneer who provides pardon for our past, peace for our present, and a promise for our future, making redemption and a new beginning possible for anyone.

versesMentioned.text: Now Deborah, a prophet, the wife of Lappidoth, was leading Israel at that time. She held court under the Palm of Deborah between Ramah and Bethel in the hill country of Ephraim, and the Israelites went up to her to have their disputes decided. She sent for Barak son of Abinoam from Kedesh in Naphtali and said to him, 'The Lord, the God of Israel, commands you: ‘Go, take with you ten thousand men of Naphtali and Zebulun and lead them up to Mount Tabor. I will lead Sisera, the commander of Jabin’s army, with his chariots and his troops to the Kishon River and give him into your hand.’' Barak said to her, 'If you go with me, I will go; but if you don’t go with me, I won’t go.' 'Certainly I will go with you,' said Deborah. 'But because of the course you are taking, the honor will not be yours, for the Lord will deliver Sisera into the hands of a woman.'

Then Deborah said to Barak, 'Go! This is the day the Lord has given Sisera into your hands. Has not the Lord gone ahead of you?' So Barak went down Mount Tabor, with ten thousand men following him. At Barak’s advance, the Lord routed Sisera and all his chariots and army by the sword, and Sisera got down from his chariot and fled on foot.
But Jael, Heber’s wife, picked up a tent peg and a hammer and went quietly to him while he lay fast asleep, exhausted. She drove the peg through his temple into the ground, and he died.

With the coming of dawn, the angels urged Lot, saying, 'Hurry! Take your wife and your two daughters who are here, or you will be swept away when the city is punished.' When he hesitated, the men grasped his hand and the hands of his wife and of his two daughters and led them safely out of the city, for the Lord was merciful to them. As soon as they had brought them out, one of them said, 'Flee for your lives! Don’t look back, and don’t stop anywhere...' But Lot’s wife looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.
However, as it is written: 'What no eye has seen, what no ear has heard, and what no human mind has conceived'— the things God has prepared for those who love him—
Humble yourselves, therefore, under God’s mighty hand, that he may lift you up in due time.

Monday.scripture: Judges 4:14 - 'Go! This is the day the Lord has given Sisera into your hands.'
Monday.focus: Identify the 'Sisera'—the main obstacle or lie—that is blocking you from your promised land.
Monday.action: Write down one specific fear, habit, or past hurt you feel God is calling you to confront.
Monday.prayer: Lord, give me clarity to see the enemy in my camp. Give me courage to believe that today is the day You deliver it into my hands.

Tuesday.scripture: Genesis 19:17 - 'Flee for your lives! Don’t look back...'
Tuesday.focus: Pioneers Don't Look Back. Decide to leave something behind.
Tuesday.action: Choose one thing from your past (a grudge, a sinful habit, a victim mentality) and symbolically turn away from it. Delete a toxic contact, throw away an old reminder, or write 'I release this' on a piece of paper and tear it up.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, I choose to walk forward on the path You've provided. Help me not to romanticize or look back at what You are calling me to leave behind.

Wednesday.scripture: 1 Peter 5:6 - 'Humble yourselves... under God’s mighty hand, that he may lift you up in due time.'
Wednesday.focus: Radical Obedience in the Present. Listen for God's whisper.
Wednesday.action: Spend 10 minutes in quiet prayer without asking for anything. Just listen. Then, obey one simple, practical prompting you sense (e.g., send an encouraging text, apologize, start a task you've avoided).
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, attune my heart to Your voice. I humble myself under Your leadership today. Give me the grace to obey Your promptings immediately, however small they seem.

Thursday.scripture: Judges 4:21 - 'Jael... picked up a tent peg and a hammer and went quietly to him...'
Thursday.focus: Pioneers Take Faith Steps. Feel the fear and do it anyway.
Thursday.action: Take one tangible, faith-filled action toward your 'promised land.' This could be having a difficult conversation, signing up for a class, setting a boundary, or making a step toward forgiveness.
Thursday.prayer: God, like Jael, I pick up the tools You've given me. I feel the fear, but I choose to act in faith. Strengthen my hands for the task You have for me.

Friday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 2:9 - 'What no eye has seen... the things God has prepared for those who love him—'
Friday.focus: God's Promise for Your Future. Dream with God.
Friday.action: Spend time imagining what your 'land flowing with milk and honey' could look like in one area of your life (family, career, spiritual growth). Write down three hopeful, God-sized possibilities.
Friday.prayer: Lord, my mind cannot conceive all You have for me. I choose to believe Your promise is bigger than my past or present. Renew my hope and expand my vision for the future You are preparing.

Saturday.action: Review your week. Where did you succeed in not looking back, being obedient, or taking a faith step? Where did you struggle? Thank God for His faithfulness in both.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, thank You for being my pioneer. You are my pardon, my peace, and my promise. Help me to remember the cost of looking back, and fix my eyes firmly on You as I move forward into all You have for me.
